remember: Students will be able to recall and state the definition of refraction, total internal reflection, and critical angle.
Students will be able to identify and explain the working of a prism and a glass slab.
Students will be able to list the different types of optical instruments and their uses.
understand: Students will be able to explain the refraction of light through a rectangular glass slab and prism.
Students will be able to describe the human eye and its defects, and explain the use of spectacles.
Students will be able to explain the working of a compound microscope and a telescope.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the refractive indices of a glass prism and a glass slab using the formulae.
Students will be able to calculate the focal length of a convex lens and a concave lens using the lens formula.
Students will be able to determine the angular magnification of a compound microscope and a telescope.
analyze: Students will be able to compare the resolving power of two different microscopes.
Students will be able to explain the limitations of a telescope and propose methods for improvement.
Students will be able to discuss the limitations of the human eye and propose ways to correct its defects.

ncert_objectives: To state the laws of refraction and their applications.
To explain the working of a prism and a glass slab.
To describe the human eye and its defects.
To explain the working of a compound microscope and a telescope.
To calculate the refractive indices of a glass prism and a glass slab using the formulae.
To calculate the focal length of a convex lens and a concave lens using the lens formula.
board_exam_objectives: Refraction of light through a rectangular glass slab and a glass prism.
Working of a compound microscope and a telescope.
Angular magnification of a compound microscope and a telescope.
